model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the tour travels directly from the first to the second, and we also track the position at which each city is visited along the route. Travelling between cities incurs a known cost that depends on the direction of travel, since the cost from one city to another need not match the cost of the reverse leg. The objective is to minimize the total travel cost of the whole tour.

sets.name: city
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single tour; the first member in the listed order plays the role of the starting depot for the ordering scheme

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost on the directed leg from the first city to the second city, in cost units; the matrix is asymmetric so the cost from one city to another need not equal the reverse, and the diagonal entries from a city to itself are zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0 so no city links to itself

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, taking a value between 0 and the number of cities minus one; used only to record visiting order so that subtours cannot form

description: Each city must be departed from exactly once. For every city, exactly one direct leg of the tour leaves it heading to some other city.

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the tour travels directly from the first to the second, and we also track the position at which each city is visited along the route. Travelling between cities incurs a known cost that depends on the direction of travel, since the cost from one city to another need not match the cost of the reverse leg. The objective is to minimize the total travel cost of the whole tour.

sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single tour; the first member in the listed order plays the role of the starting depot for the ordering scheme

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost on the directed leg from the first city to the second city, in cost units; the matrix is asymmetric so the cost from one city to another need not equal the reverse, and the diagonal entries from a city to itself are zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0 so no city links to itself

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, taking a value between 0 and the number of cities minus one; used only to record visiting order so that subtours cannot form

description: Each city must be arrived at exactly once. For every city, exactly one direct leg of the tour comes into it from some other city.

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the tour travels directly from the first to the second, and we also track the position at which each city is visited along the route. Travelling between cities incurs a known cost that depends on the direction of travel, since the cost from one city to another need not match the cost of the reverse leg. The objective is to minimize the total travel cost of the whole tour.

sets.name: city
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single tour; the first member in the listed order plays the role of the starting depot for the ordering scheme

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost on the directed leg from the first city to the second city, in cost units; the matrix is asymmetric so the cost from one city to another need not equal the reverse, and the diagonal entries from a city to itself are zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0 so no city links to itself

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, taking a value between 0 and the number of cities minus one; used only to record visiting order so that subtours cannot form

description: The visiting positions and the chosen legs must rule out any smaller loop that fails to include the starting city, so that the route forms one single tour through all the cities rather than several disconnected cycles. For each ordered pair of distinct cities other than the starting city, whenever the tour goes directly from the first to the second the second city's position along the route must come after the first city's position. When that direct leg is not taken the relationship between their positions is left unrestricted. The starting city is exempt from this position bookkeeping.
expected_pyomo:
def mtz_rule(model, i, j):
    first = list(model.city)[0]
    n = len(model.city)
    if i == j or i == first or j == first:
        return Constraint.Skip
    return model.u[i] - model.u[j] + n * model.x[i, j] <= n - 1
model.mtz = Constraint(model.city, model.city, rule=mtz_rule)

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the tour travels directly from the first to the second, and we also track the position at which each city is visited along the route. Travelling between cities incurs a known cost that depends on the direction of travel, since the cost from one city to another need not match the cost of the reverse leg. The objective is to minimize the total travel cost of the whole tour.

sets.name: city
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single tour; the first member in the listed order plays the role of the starting depot for the ordering scheme

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost on the directed leg from the first city to the second city, in cost units; the matrix is asymmetric so the cost from one city to another need not equal the reverse, and the diagonal entries from a city to itself are zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0 so no city links to itself

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, taking a value between 0 and the number of cities minus one; used only to record visiting order so that subtours cannot form

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, every city must be departed from exactly once, with a single direct leg of the tour leaving it toward some other city. Second, every city must be arrived at exactly once, with a single direct leg of the tour entering it from some other city. Finally, the visiting positions and the chosen legs must prevent any smaller loop that excludes the starting city, so that for each ordered pair of distinct cities other than the starting city, taking the direct leg from the first to the second forces the second city's position along the route to come after the first city's position, while the starting city is left out of this position bookkeeping.
